---
title: "Tutorials"
subtitle: "Task-focused guides for the typed fmrimod modeling workflow."
---

These guides are organized around the public fmrimod seam, not around a
function-by-function port of the R packages:

```text
fmri_dataset -> fmri_lm -> contrast -> group_fit
```

Some pages use compact matrix helpers because they are useful numerical
canaries. Flagship workflow pages should prefer typed datasets, typed specs,
first-level fits, contrast objects, and group-level results.

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Overview and workflow](overview-workflow.qmd)
Walk through the modeling stack: sampling frames, datasets, typed specs,
baseline terms, GLM fitting, contrasts, and group summaries.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Build a design matrix](design-matrices.qmd)
Create condition-specific HRF regressors from an event table and inspect the
resulting column metadata.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Parametric modulators](parametric-modulators.qmd)
Model conditions modulated by trial-by-trial continuous variables. Centering
semantics, the `center_modulators=` opt-out, and why fmrimod does not
orthogonalize modulators automatically (Mumford et al. 2015).
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Contrasts](contrasts.qmd)
Create named contrast vectors from model columns and use factorial contrast
generators for main effects and interactions.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Simulation](simulation.qmd)
Generate clean BOLD responses, fMRI-like noise, and matrix time series for
method checks.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Linear model](linear-model.qmd)
Fit a small first-level model through `fmri_lm()`, then inspect beta,
t-statistic, and contrast outputs.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Datasets](datasets.qmd)
Represent in-memory and latent data as canonical `fmrimod.dataset` objects with
sampling frames and event tables.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Group analysis](group-analysis.qmd)
Summarize first-level estimates with native group-analysis and meta-regression
helpers.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Functional connectivity](functional-connectivity.qmd)
Use residualization and Fisher z helpers for auxiliary effect-size summaries.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Sketched and AR-aware models](sketched-ar-models.qmd)
Use low-rank GLM sketches and residual autocorrelation diagnostics for large
or temporally correlated data.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Plugin development](plugin-development.qmd)
Extend internal registries while keeping user-facing modeling decisions typed.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [Benchmark datasets](benchmark-datasets.qmd)
Load deterministic fixtures, build design matrices, and score estimated
condition betas against known truth.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .p-3}
### [API reference](../reference/index.qmd)
Look up the main event, HRF, contrast, GLM, and single-trial APIs generated
from Python docstrings.
:::
:::

:::
